Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-11-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted it's tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL W. VALLEY'S HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST CAN BE PROTECTED BY RECOVERY OF MONEY DAMAGES SHOULD PLAINTIFF PREVAILS. INJUNCTION WOULD PREVENT DEFENDANT FROM EXPLOITING OPPORTUNITY TO SUBSTANTIAL DETRIMENT OF DEFENDANT AND IF PLAINTIFF PREVAILS, ALSO TO PLAINTIFF. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (302/PB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

Notice Of Motion & Motion To Quash Service Of O.S.C.
Off Calendar Nov-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Briscoe of Briscoe Ivester & Bazel, LLP, 402-2700, appearing for plaintiff and responding party
James B. Little, 408-999-0925, appearing for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS TIMOTHY NOBRIGA and SOUTHERN CROSS MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS' Motion To Quash Service Of OSC is granted. Compliance with CCP 413.10(c) and 415.40 not proved by plaintiff; see CRC 359(a). In any event, "I caused" is insufficient to establish service and email service is not provided for. Order signed in open Court. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Nov-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Briscoe of Briscoe Ivester & Bazel, LLP, 402-2700, appearing for plaintiff and moving party
James B. Little, 408-999-0925, appearing for defendants and responding parties

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Michael Valley's Order to Show Cause re: Preliminary Injunction is off calendar as moot. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
